content:
Most Angular Applications Don’t Become Difficult Because Of Angular

3 min read
3 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Developers often blame complexity on:

• Too many components

• Too many services

• Too much state

• Too many dependencies
Those problems certainly exist.
But after reviewing many enterprise Angular applications, I’ve noticed something more fundamental.
The real issue usually isn’t the framework.
It’s that nobody clearly defined where one feature ends and another begins.
Poor boundaries quietly create complexity.
Not a Member? Read for FREE here.
Components Are Easy To Build
Creating a component is simple.
CustomerCardComponentAdding another one is also simple.
CustomerListComponentAngular makes building features enjoyable.
The challenge starts when those features begin talking to each other.
The Invisible Problem
